paragraphs.text: SRD-46 supports a **chemistry-focused comparison** of iron and copper mainly through their common oxidation states and shared ligands.

paragraphs.text:
## Coverage in SRD-46

| Metal species | SRD-46 metal_id | Card count |
|---|---:|---:|
| Cu²⁺ | metal_41 | 8963 |
| Cu⁺ | metal_42 | 285 |
| Fe³⁺ | metal_61 | 1473 |
| Fe²⁺ | metal_62 | 667 |

This shows **much broader coverage for copper, especially Cu²⁺**, than for iron.

claims.text: **much broader coverage for copper, especially Cu²⁺**, than for iron.

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.
Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.

paragraphs.text:
## Shared-ligand pattern

From the shared Fe/Cu ligand set, SRD-46 indicates:

| Ligand example | Fe cards | Cu cards | Pattern |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Hydroxide | 51 | 37 | Fe-favored |
| Ammonia | 32 | 55 | Cu-favored |
| Thiocyanic acid | 41 | 25 | Fe-favored |
| Histidine | 7 | 53 | Strongly Cu-favored |
| Acetic acid | 17 | 37 | Cu-favored |
| Glycine | 20 | 31 | Cu-favored |
| EDTA | 20 | 20 | Near parity |
| Citric acid | 19 | 19 | Near parity |

Overall, **Cu has more entries for most shared ligands**, while **Fe stands out more for hydroxide and thiocyanate chemistry**.

claims.text: From the shared Fe/Cu ligand set, SRD-46 indicates:
**Cu has more entries for most shared ligands**
**Fe stands out more for hydroxide and thiocyanate chemistry**

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.
WHERE c.metal_id IN (41,42,61,62) GROUP BY c.ligand_id, c.ligand_name_SRD HAVING fe_cards > 0 AND cu_cards > 0

tool_snippets.snippet: SELECT c.ligand_id, c.ligand_name_SRD, SUM(CASE WHEN c.metal_id IN (61,62) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fe_cards, SUM(CASE WHEN c.metal_id IN (41,42) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS cu_cards, COUNT(*) AS total_cards FROM ligandmetal_card c WHERE c.metal_id IN (41,42,61,62) GROUP BY c.ligand_id, c.ligand_name_SRD HAV

paragraphs.text:
## Thermodynamic depth

For **ammonia** and **2,2'-bipyridyl**, SRD-46 contains not just logK but also some **ΔH** and **ΔS** data, though this is much sparser than logK.

For **EDTA**, supporting thermodynamic data were found for:

| Pair | ΔH present? | VLM count | β entries | T range (°C) | Ionic strength range (M) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|
| Cu²⁺–EDTA | Yes | 9 | 3 | 15–35 | 0–1 |
| Fe³⁺–EDTA | Yes | 4 | 4 | 25 | 0.1–1 |
| Fe²⁺–EDTA | Yes | 2 | 2 | 25 | 0.1 |

claims.text: For **ammonia** and **2,2'-bipyridyl**, SRD-46 contains not just logK but also some **ΔH** and **ΔS** data
though this is much sparser than logK.

paragraphs.text:
## Bottom line

| Aspect | Iron | Copper |
|---|---|---|
| Database coverage | Moderate, mainly Fe³⁺ and Fe²⁺ | Much larger, dominated by Cu²⁺ |
| Shared-ligand breadth | Good, but fewer records | Broader and denser |
| Typical standout ligands in retrieved data | Hydroxide, thiocyanate | Ammonia, histidine, bipyridyl |
| Oxidation-state spread in retrieved data | Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺ | Cu²⁺ dominant, Cu⁺ present but much smaller |

**Concise comparison:** in SRD-46, **copper is better represented and more extensively characterized than iron**, especially for classic N-donor ligands. **Iron shows comparatively stronger emphasis in hydroxo and thiocyanate systems**, while **copper dominates ammonia- and heteroaromatic-N donor coordination chemistry**.

If you want, I can next do a **matched logK comparison for one common ligand** such as **EDTA, ammonia, bipyridyl, glycine, or citrate** using the closest available conditions in SRD-46.

| Oxidation-state spread in retrieved data | Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺ | Cu²⁺ dominant, Cu⁺ present but much smaller |
**copper is better represented and more extensively characterized than iron**
**Iron shows comparatively stronger emphasis in hydroxo and thiocyanate systems**
**copper dominates ammonia- and heteroaromatic-N donor coordination chemistry**
